expectations.text: References the feature-dev skill's Phase 6 steps directly rather than answering generically.
expectations.evidence: Response opens: 'The feature-dev workflow's Phase 6 explicitly requires two separate, independent subagents in a specific order.' Phase 6 Actions 1 and 2 are quoted directly by name and content.

expectations.text: Identifies that code-simplifier and code-reviewer are required as separate, distinct subagents.
expectations.evidence: Lists Phase 6 required actions: '1. Always launch an independent code-simplifier subagent' and '2. Always run independent code-reviewer agents' — both named as separate items with the 'always' qualifier.

expectations.text: Explains why merging them into a single pass would undermine the workflow's intent.
expectations.evidence: Section 'Why Merging Them Undermines the Workflow' explains that code-simplifier and code-reviewer serve 'distinct, non-overlapping purposes' and that 'running a single combined agent collapses these lenses.' Also explains that merging destroys the ordering guarantee.

expectations.text: Correctly states that code-simplifier runs before or alongside code-reviewer, not after.
expectations.evidence: 'The code-simplifier runs before the code-reviewer pass.' Sequence shown explicitly: 'Implementation → code-simplifier (refactor pass) → code-reviewer(s) (quality review)'.

expectations.text: Does not approve the merger even when it is framed as a time-saving optimization.
expectations.evidence: 'Reject the proposal.' and 'collapsing the code-simplifier and code-reviewer into a single agent is not among the acceptable reductions' — stated explicitly despite the time-saving framing. Acceptable alternatives listed do not include merging.
